nodes.label: segmented-control promotion revisit — design-only evaluation + proposed ADR (Kim's 2026-07-07 ask vs the Kim-ratified ADR-0086)
evidence base — the ratified record, the shipped mechanism, the consumer inventory
ADR-0086 ground truth (Context / Decision / Alternatives / ratification history read in full)
nodes.accept: the analysis quotes ADR-0086's own 'new ui-segmented component' rejection (0086 Alternatives, first bullet) and its ratification row (Kim, 2026-07-06, ratify & build) — verifiable by citation

nodes.label: shipped mechanism facts (radio-group.ts state seam + orientation resolution; radio-group.css tag-keyed selectors + --ui-radio-group-* token chain)
nodes.accept: every mechanism claim in the report carries a file:line citation (radio-group.ts #writeIndexCount:202-205, connected():86-89; radio-group.css [3]/[4] blocks:55-224)

nodes.label: consumer inventory of ui-radio-group[variant=segmented] (site knob UI, a2ui catalog schema, tests, docs, dimensions.css doctrine comment; corpus/transcripts negative)
nodes.accept: the report enumerates every consumer with a path, and states the corpus/transcript NEGATIVE with the grep that proved it

nodes.label: the for/against analysis (both directions argued from the evidence, neither rubber-stamped)
steelman of ADR-0086 (does the behavioral-identity + reuse reasoning still hold against the shipped code?)
nodes.accept: the steelman is grounded in cited shipped behavior (exclusivity/roving/commit/form-value/reset all live in radio-group.ts) and states plainly whether anything since ratification falsifies it

nodes.label: case for promotion (discoverability/naming evidence, semantic leaks of the radio model, children-tag readability, future-divergence pressure)
nodes.accept: each promotion argument is either grounded in a cited fact or explicitly marked as a taste/API-surface judgment reserved for Kim; no bare assertions

nodes.label: middle-path feasibility costing — a thin ui-segmented-control over the SAME machinery, costed against THIS fleet's real component surface (descriptor/gallery/exports/size-gate/browser suites/a2ui wire/CSS keying/private-seam names)
nodes.accept: the costing names the concrete blockers by file:line (#writeIndexCount is private; --ui-radio-group-* names baked into CSS; gallery derives 1:1 from descriptor tags) and lands a day-sized estimate

nodes.label: recommendation + the fork for Kim (one call, named re-open triggers, decision-shaped fork)
nodes.accept: the report names exactly ONE recommendation, >=2 named re-open triggers, and the fork stated as two ratify-able branches with their costs

nodes.label: proposed ADR-0092 (the correct vehicle per .claude/docs/adr/README.md's three-way test; Status: proposed, never accepted; index row)
nodes.accept: python ~/.claude/skills/adr-author/scripts/adr_check.py .claude/docs/adr/0092-*.md exits 0; Status is 'proposed'; the README index gains the 0092 row; ADR-0086 itself is NOT edited

nodes.label: build-sequencing note (both branches sized honestly — the keep-and-position slice AND the full promotion/migration wave)
nodes.accept: both branches carry an estimate with the touched surface enumerated (files/seats/gates), including the promotion branch's consumer-migration story (knob UI, a2ui catalog, tests, docs)

nodes.label: handoff block (handoff-compose contract back to the coordinator; doc-reviewer named as ratifier's critic)
nodes.accept: the final report carries all eight handoff fields in order, Status on line 1, gates named by command with verdicts, unrun gates stated UNMEASURED

actions.label: verify ADR-0086's behavioral-identity claim against the shipped code
enumerate every current consumer of variant=segmented (and prove the negatives)
ground the revisit in the ratified record (cite the 0086 Alternatives rejection + ratification history honestly)
articulate the promotion case at full strength
cost the thin-wrapper/composition path against the fleet's real per-component surface
make one concrete recommendation with named re-open triggers
apply the amendment-vs-supersession-vs-extension test correctly and pick the ADR vehicle
author the proposed ADR (never self-ratified) + index row
size the build sequencing for BOTH branches, including consumer migration
surface the fork for Kim's ruling, decision-shaped
compose the handoff per the handoff-compose contract

edges.why: the steelman argues FROM 0086's own recorded Context/Alternatives — it cannot be written before the record is read in full
whether the reuse claim still holds is checked against the shipped radio-group.ts/.css, not the ADR's self-description
the middle-path cost turns on mechanism facts (private #writeIndexCount, tag-keyed CSS, baked token names) only the code read supplies
the promotion case's semantic-leak and discoverability arguments are weighed against WHERE segmented is actually consumed today
the migration story enumerates exactly the consumer inventory — it cannot be sized before the inventory exists
the recommendation must answer the steelman, not bypass it
the recommendation must answer the promotion case at full strength, not a strawman
the recommendation picks among costed options; the middle-path costing is an input, not a footnote
the ADR records the recommendation's position; the vehicle choice (extension vs supersession) follows from WHICH recommendation stands
the sequencing note sizes the recommended branch first and the fork's other branch against it
the handoff routes on the authored ADR's path + gate verdicts
the handoff carries the sequencing estimate the coordinator routes on
